summary: Frontend-only validation of LithoForge's new expanded printer/filament/nozzle catalogs feature on /studio. Verified printer searchable combobox, nozzle-by-printer filtering, layer-height window/auto-snap logic, nozzle warning hints, printer-switch nozzle auto-snap, filament library material chips + PETG badge, and end-to-end generation. All tests passed against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://color-match-slicer.preview.emergentagent.com — no bugs, no console errors.
verified_scenarios: Default load: trigger='Generic FDM (OrcaSlicer)', nozzle=0.40mm, layer-height-range-hint='0.40 mm nozzle → 0.10–0.32 mm layer window (25–80% of Ø).'

Printer combobox: typing 'kobra' filters group to 4 Anycubic Kobra models; selecting config-printer-select-option-anycubic_kobra3 updates trigger to 'Anycubic Kobra 3 Combo (ACE Pro) · AMS/MMU' (multi_tool=true rendered amber).
Selecting 0.6 → nozzle-detail-hint='Large nozzle — fine image detail will be lost in the print.' with amber-400 color class.
Switching to Bambu A1 (nozzles [0.2,0.4,0.6,0.8]) → selecting 0.2 → hint='Small nozzle — maximum detail, but much longer print time.'; layer-height auto-snaps to 0.16 (within 0.05–0.16 window).
Switching Bambu A1 → Creality K1 (nozzles=[0.4,0.6,0.8]) auto-snaps nozzle back to 0.40mm (verified trigger text).
Layer-height select for 0.4mm nozzle lists exactly 0.10/0.12/0.16/0.20/0.24/0.28/0.32 (= 0.10–0.32 window, the 25–80% rule).

FilamentLibraryDialog opens via swatch edit pencil → 'Match from manufacturer'. Material chips: ALL/PLA/PETG (library-material-all/pla/petg). PETG filter yields 15 SKUs all carrying a sky-blue PETG badge (bg-sky-950 / text-sky-300 / border-sky-800). 20 brand chips render (close to the ~19 stated).

End-to-end generation: programmatic 16x16 PNG uploaded via the file input → GENERATE button clicked → preview viewport renders 'ΔE 65.14 · 22 layers · 6 filaments' with LAYER STACK populated and 3MF download button present (gated by 'Sign in to download', expected for anon).
Regression: render-mode toggle (LITHOPHANE/PAINTING), geometry-select (4 options), width-slider (100→171mm via keyboard), AI palette suggest-palette-btn present.
critical_code_review_comments: PaletteEditor.jsx:354 — AddTile (data-testid=palette-add-btn) is only rendered when filaments.length<8. Default config seeds 8 filaments, so the '+' button is invisible at first load and only the swatch-edit-pencil → 'Match from manufacturer' path opens FilamentLibraryDialog. The user-facing prompt for this test said 'open via palette + button (or swatch edit)' — only the swatch-edit path is reachable in the default empty-image state. Consider always rendering an Add slot (disabled with tooltip when full) for discoverability.

NozzleSelect.jsx:60 — useEffect that snaps the nozzle on profile-change deliberately omits `value` from deps via eslint-disable. This is correct (it only snaps when the printer changes, not when the user picks a value) but the disable comment could explain WHY for future readers.

ConfigPanel.jsx:67 — ALL_LAYER_HEIGHTS hardcoded; the 25–80% window for very-large nozzles (e.g. 0.8mm → 0.20–0.64) currently has no >0.32 entries, so the slider quietly tops out at 0.32 even though 0.40/0.48/0.64 are legal. Either extend the list or document the cap.
FilamentLibraryDialog renders 20 brand chips in the 'Find by color' tab (request mentioned ~19) — verify whether that's intended or one duplicate brand snuck in.
Anonymous users on /studio see preview generation but cannot download (correct per /app/memory/test_credentials.md gating). The 'Sign in to download' nudge in the header is clear.
test_credentials: Anonymous flow — no auth needed for any scenario in scope. 'My library' tab + mine-material-select was NOT exercised in authed mode (would require seeding user_sessions per /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Mark this as an explicit gap, although source inspection confirms data-testid=mine-material-select is wired with PLA/PETG options at FilamentLibraryDialog.jsx:447.
seed_data_creation: Created a 16x16 procedurally generated PNG in /tmp for the upload+generate flow. No DB writes. All client-only state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tested via public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Key takeaways: (a) palette-add-btn isn't in the DOM by default because the seeded palette already has 8 filaments — open the library via [data-testid=edit-filament-<name>] → [data-testid=open-manufacturer-library-<name>]; (b) creality_k1 has no 0.2mm nozzle, so it's the correct printer for the auto-snap regression; (c) generation request listener attached after navigation may miss the /generate POST — confirm success via the preview text 'ΔE NN.NN · N layers · N filaments' which appears within ~8s; (d) anonymous downloads gated by sign-in, expected.
